title: Response to Audit Logging Failures and Forwarder Gaps

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `_internal` sourcetype=`splunkd` OR `http_event_collector_metrics`, deployment server phonehome metrics
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=_internal earliest=-4h (sourcetype=splunkd OR sourcetype=http_event_collector_metrics)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count(eval(match(_raw,"(?i)blocked|pause|queue|full|failure"))) as failure_signals,
        dc(host) as hosts by _time, sourcetype
| where failure_signals>0
| sort - failure_signals
description: Correlates forwarder offline events, TCP routing failures, and HEC 4xx/5xx spikes so operators can respond before AU-5 audit logging failures silently drop evidence.
value: Correlates forwarder offline events, TCP routing failures, and HEC 4xx/5xx spikes so operators can respond before AU-5 audit logging failures silently drop evidence.
grandmaExplanation: Correlates agent offline events, TCP routing failures, and 4xx/5xx spikes so operators can respond before AU-5 audit logging failures silently drop evidence ŌĆö so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Universal Forwarder / Splunk Cloud Platform.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` sourcetype=`splunkd` OR `http_event_collector_metrics`, deployment server phonehome metrics.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
(1) Enable verbose forwarder logging only on troubleshooting windows to avoid noise; (2) route `_internal` to a security operations index with RBAC; (3) alert on sustained `failure_signals`; (4) document compensating controls when maintenance pauses logging.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal earliest=-4h (sourcetype=splunkd OR sourcetype=http_event_collector_metrics)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count(eval(match(_raw,"(?i)blocked|pause|queue|full|failure"))) as failure_signals,
        dc(host) as hosts by _time, sourcetype
| where failure_signals>0
| sort - failure_signals
```

Understanding this SPL

**Response to Audit Logging Failures and Forwarder Gaps (AU-5)** ŌĆö Correlates forwarder offline events, TCP routing failures, and HEC 4xx/5xx spikes so operators can respond before AU-5 audit logging failures silently drop evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` sourcetype=`splunkd` OR `http_event_collector_metrics`, deployment server phonehome metrics. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Universal Forwarder / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal; **sourcetype**: splunkd, http_event_collector_metrics. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=_internal, sourcetype=splunkd, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where failure_signals>0` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (failure signals), Table (top hosts), Alert list (spikes)
visualization: Time chart (failure signals), Table (top hosts), Alert list (spikes)
